summary: Small rectangular single-story building with a stepped brick barrel-like roof, sandstone upper walls with glass windows in a continuous band, stone corner pillars, and a raised white border foundation surrounding a wooden base trim.

shape.overall_form: elongated rectangle with low pitched stepped barrel roof

elements: continuous horizontal band of windows on long sides (3-4 panes per side segment)
stepped brick roof rising in center with stone slab caps along steps
stone (dark) corner pillars at each end
wooden lower trim between foundation and sandstone walls
rebuild_hints: Footprint: rectangular platform approx 7x15 blocks inside white border; build a 1-block high wooden skirting around base then sandstone walls above.
Place a continuous row of glass windows centered on the wall height (long sides) separated by sandstone blocks; add dark stone corner pillars at both short ends extending up to the white trim height.
Make the roof by layering brick blocks in stepped rows along the long axis to form a low barrel/hip shape; cap each step edge with stone slabs.
Surround the building with a one-block-high white (quartz) border flush to ground forming a shallow trough around the structure.
uncertainties: Exact block variants: sandstone vs smooth sandstone vs chiseled sandstone uncertain (labeled as 'sandstone').
White border material appears like quartz but could be smooth stone or another white block; labeled 'quartz' with moderate confidence.
Precise interior layout and floor material not visible; wooden skirting assumed from visible texture but could be another wood type.
Exact footprint dimensions estimated from images; counts may be off by a few blocks (±1-2).
